← the Atlas

Workflow viewer

plan · budding ·JUN 01, 2026

Integrating Claude Code dynamic-workflow JSONL into kolu — surfacing fan-out runs on the tile chrome and an Inspector run-list with live timelines.

Contents

  • Multiplicity is the normal case (verified)
  • What a dynamic workflow writes, and where status lives
  • Proposal — two phases, each shippable alone
    • Phase 1 — repoint the reader (name + fan-out count appear)
    • Phase 2 — a list of workflow runs with live timelines, in the Inspector tab
  • What the hickey + lowy pass changed
  • Cut line

Analysis of a live do-wf run in drishti/.worktrees/hm, generalized to the real case: a session holds many workflow runs at once — some running, some finished. Two-phase proposal, revised after hickey + lowy.

Headline

kolu already knows a workflow is running — it just can’t say which one or how many agents. The running state works (it comes from the transcript, not the journal). The enrichment is what’s broken: deriveWorkflowProgress() reads a journal file that no longer exists, returns null, so the name/fan-out badge stays dark. And the model is single-run — even fixed, it collapses a session’s whole history to one entry. (verified: core.ts:493-552 reads one dead workflows/<runId>.json; state is set separately at core.ts:378-385.)

Precisely what fails

deriveState promotes waiting → running_background purely from the main-transcript launch marker via outstandingBackgroundTasks() — no journal read (core.ts:378-385). So the spinner + “Running in background” label already render. The journal is read only to fill the workflow field (session-watcher.ts:245-247), and agentWorkflow() needs both state===running_background and workflow!=null (agentDisplay.ts:43-44). State is true; workflow is null → the do-wf · 5 agents badge and the “Workflow” inspector row are the only things dark.

Multiplicity is the normal case (verified)

SessionWorkflow runsFan-out
agency/…/cc-workflow2 runs (wf_84c9119d, wf_e6f94ccf)4 agents, 14 agents
kolu/…/modest-runner3 runs—
drishti/…/hm (live)1 run, in flight6 started / 5 done

So the UI target is a list of runs per session, each with its own status and timeline — not a single badge value.

What a dynamic workflow writes, and where status lives

~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session>.jsonl              # main transcript — launch markers + completion notifications
~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/<session>/
├── workflows/scripts/<name>-<runId>.js                       # persisted script — meta.name + meta.phases[]
└── subagents/workflows/
    ├── wf_84c9119d-127/  journal.jsonl + agent-*.jsonl       # run 1
    └── wf_e6f94ccf-a1f/  journal.jsonl + agent-*.jsonl       # run 2 …

The three discrepancies vs. kolu’s current reader, all verified:

kolu assumesOn disk
One journal at <session>/workflows/<runId>.jsonMany at <session>/subagents/workflows/<runId>/journal.jsonl
Snapshot object {workflowName,status,agentCount}Event log: {type:"started",agentId} / {type:"result",agentId,result:{status,…}}
Name + status live in the journalname only in the script meta; workflow-level status is NOT in the journal at all

The subtle one — status is a transcript fact, not a journal fact

The journal records per-agent started/result, never a “workflow finished” event. In the live drishti run, 5 agents resulted while the workflow is still going (between phases). So “all started agents resulted ⇒ completed” reports false completions between phases. The authoritative signal is in the main transcript: a <task-notification> carrying <status>completed</status>, keyed by the launch Task ID, linked to the Run ID in the same launch marker. kolu already pairs Task↔Run IDs at core.ts:419, 478.

Proposal — two phases, each shippable alone

Phase 1 — repoint the reader (name + fan-out count appear)

Bug-fix-shaped, almost entirely in core.ts; no UI change. They already consume ClaudeWorkflow {name,status,agents} (agentDisplay.ts:42-44, TerminalMeta.tsx:78-80, MetadataInspector.tsx:194-205):

  • Point the journal path at subagents/workflows/<runId>/journal.jsonl.
  • Read the event log: agents = distinct started agentIds; name from the script meta.
  • Status comes from the transcript, not the journal. A run is running iff its taskId is still in outstandingBackgroundTasks(); completed/failed once its terminal <task-notification> is seen. No new “all-agents-done” heuristic.

Phase 2 — a list of workflow runs with live timelines, in the Inspector tab

Rendered prototype — a session’s runs, newest first; running auto-expanded, finished collapsed to a summary you can open:

Inspector
Code
Kindclaude-code
Staterunning in background
Workflows3 runs · 1 running
do-wfrunning6 agents · 4m elapsed▾
Sync · Research · Implementdone
Checkstatic-correctness gate
a9b4…61c0
…
Docs · Format · Commit · Review · …9 phases pending
deep-researchcompleted14 agents · 6m12s▸
finished 12m ago · click to expand timeline & per-agent transcripts
do-wffailed4 agents · 1m03s▸
failed at Check (1 agent failed) · click to expand
▸ click an agent → opens its transcript (reuses parseClaudeCodeJsonl → TranscriptEvent[] renderer)

Wiring (shaped by the review pass below):

  • On-demand fetch, returning a list. A new RPC loadWorkflowRuns(session) enumerates subagents/workflows/wf_*/ and returns WorkflowRun[] — each {runId, name, status, startedEpoch, endedEpoch, agents[]}. Mirrors the existing exportTranscriptHtml/loadClaudeCodeTranscript pull pattern.
  • Live metadata stays scalar. ClaudeCodeInfo still carries only the single running-run summary (plus optionally a small “N runs” count) — the full list is never on the firehose.
  • Per-agent transcript reuses the canonical seam. agent-<id>.jsonl is a normal transcript → parseClaudeCodeJsonl() → TranscriptEvent[] → existing kolu-transcript-html renderer. No parallel event schema.

What the hickey + lowy pass changed

Reuse the transcript IR seam (lowy headline)

Claude JSONL is already normalized to TranscriptEvent[] with sub-agent boundaries. The per-agent transcript view consumes that; only the journal (a distinct runtime format) gets its own tiny reader.

Keep ClaudeWorkflow scalar; never put the run list on live metadata

A list of full runs on the firehose would bloat every metadata tick and multiply the unenforced “workflow non-null only while running_background” invariant already split across a write gate and a read gate. Fetched on demand instead.

No new right-panel tab (lowy)

RightPanelTabKindSchema = z.enum(["inspector","code"]) is a wire contract with persisted activeTab + session restore — adding a kind is a protocol change. The run list lives inside the existing Inspector tab.

Two honest gaps

(1) The journal doesn’t tag each agent with its phase — grouping is inferred from start order against meta.phases, reliable for a sequential pipeline like do-wf but not arbitrary parallel() fan-out. Fallback: a flat agent timeline + the declared phase strip; the real fix is the runtime stamping phase into the journal (upstream). (2) Status for an old completed run needs the terminal notification, which may have scrolled out of the transcript tail — so the status scan must read the full transcript (or treat dir-present + not-outstanding as completed).

Cut line

Phase 1 alone is worth shipping: a small correction that makes an already-built surface work for the first time, showing the active run. Phase 2 adds the multi-run list + timelines and is independently reviewable. When ready, drive it with /do.
